title: DarkLense AI NHI Corpus Assessment
executiveSummary: DarkLense indexes 645 LLM-paraphrased knowledge cards from 86 videos. AI NHI assessed all 645 cards for universal human value: 15 Tier-S essentials, 20 Tier-A high-value picks across How Influence Works, Emotional Triggers & Boundaries, Recognize & Resist Manipulation, and related themes. Cards are standalone defensive literacy — not raw transcripts. Use for recognition, boundaries, media literacy, and ethical self-governance — not coercion.

pillars.name: Psychology
pillars.description: Identity, emotion, behavior, neuroscience, persuasion mechanics, frames, and interpersonal influence at the individual level.

pillars.name: Psychological Warfare
pillars.description: Psyops, propaganda, division strategies, cognitive virology, fear control, and population-level behavioral manipulation.

pillars.name: Information Operations
pillars.description: Media manipulation, algorithmic influence, narrative warfare, manufactured consensus, and information-environment defense.

criticalWarnings.title: Dementia Erases Your Identity
criticalWarnings.content: Dementia removes random pieces of your life—memories, names, faces, and sense of home—until familiar people become strangers and you watch yourself disappear while the world barely notices. This gradu

criticalWarnings.title: The Immunity Illusion
criticalWarnings.content: Believing you are too smart or aware to join a cult lowers your guard and makes you more vulnerable to recruitment than someone who stays alert.

criticalWarnings.title: Preemptive Invalidation
criticalWarnings.content: Prepare recruits for outsiders' concerns by framing family and friends as afraid, stuck in the old system, or unable to understand. When criticism arrives it feels predictable and dismissible.

criticalWarnings.title: Why Lives Stop Changing After 25
criticalWarnings.content: Around age 25 the brain decides it has seen enough and locks your identity in place. It then rejects new inputs that don't align, causing most people's lives to plateau despite remaining potential.

criticalWarnings.title: Manufactured Artificial Consensus
criticalWarnings.content: Bots and coordinated accounts simulate mass disapproval or support, making artificial opinions appear as organic public sentiment. This bottom-up approach disguises top-down control as everyday consen

criticalWarnings.title: Unrestricted Warfare Division Strategy
criticalWarnings.content: Adversaries splinter societies by fragmenting ideologies into opposing factions and flooding them with tribal language. The goal is to make groups fight each other rather than recognize shared interes

criticalWarnings.title: Reactions Leak Power
criticalWarnings.content: Every reaction leaks power, every explanation invites judgment, and every justification causes you to shrink. Replace these habits with emotional discipline to preserve composure and authority.

criticalWarnings.title: Lingering Internal Conflict
criticalWarnings.content: Even under heavy hypnotic fog and compliance, a small internal part may still register disagreement and attempt to surface physically as nausea or unease.

criticalWarnings.title: Scarcity Triggers Impulsive Decisions
criticalWarnings.content: Talking or thinking about scarcity—running out of money, resources, or time—activates the primitive brain and forces irrational, impulsive choices. Counter it immediately by visualizing concrete oppor

criticalWarnings.title: Rank Is Not Leadership
criticalWarnings.content: Formal position or rank does not equal leadership. People follow the strongest, clearest, most consistent behavioral signals in any environment, not the person with the brass plaque or highest title.

criticalWarnings.title: Ego Is the Only Enemy
criticalWarnings.content: The ego is a protective story built from trauma, fear, and conditioning that demands separation, comparison, and validation to survive. It blocks truth because it cannot exist in the presence of onene

criticalWarnings.title: The Cost of Performance
criticalWarnings.content: Spending life curating a perfect image for others is pointless. They remember only your failures because those help them feel better about themselves. Show up honestly instead of performing.

ethicalUse.note: DarkLense is defensive literacy — understanding influence to resist it, not to weaponize it.

humanValueAssessment.title: AI NHI — Universal Human Value Assessment
humanValueAssessment.methodology: Full-corpus NHI scan of all 645 knowledge cards. Each card scored on manipulation defense, identity/autonomy, emotional boundaries, media literacy, persuasion awareness, relationship dynamics, warning salience, and framework actionability. Tier S = essential for every civilian; Tier A = high universal value; Tier B = strong situational value. Manual NHI boosts applied to cult-warning, psyop-detection, and algorithmic-identity cards.

executiveSummaryDistill.lead: NHI assessed all 645 cards for universal human importance — not operator utility, not sport persuasion, not niche elite tradecraft.
findings: 85 warning cards — highest salience for civilians who assume they are immune
89 frameworks — 15 Tier-S models are actionable without classified context
Manipulation defense and identity/autonomy dominate the top 60 — not tactical pickup or sales scripts
Media literacy and algorithmic identity cards rank essential in the digital-attention economy
Defensive literacy stack holds: detect psyops → decode frames/triggers → defend boundaries → discipline attention
executiveSummaryDistill.tierSTitle: Tier S — essential for every human (15 cards)
tierS: The FATE Model — Recognize & Resist Manipulation

Compliance Teaches People to Hide — How Influence Works
imperative: Assume you are already being influenced — immunity is the primary vulnerability
Identity is the attack surface; protect self-authorship before debating facts
Triggers precede compliance — decode emotion before accepting frames
Algorithms curate identity; outrage is engagement fuel, not truth signal
Use frameworks for recognition and exit — never for coercion

themes.label: Emotional Triggers & Boundaries
themes.why: Unmanaged triggers and absent boundaries make anyone exploitable when stressed or shamed.

themes.label: How Influence Works
themes.why: Understanding persuasion mechanics is defensive literacy — the vaccine, not the weapon.

themes.label: Identity, Autonomy & Self-Authorship
themes.why: Identity is the control surface — lose authorship of your story and you accept frames others set for you.

themes.label: Relationships & Social Power
themes.why: Status, rapport, and power leaks shape family, work, and community — everyone navigates these daily.

themes.label: Media & Information Literacy
themes.why: Algorithms, outrage loops, and manufactured consensus target every person with a screen.

themes.label: Recognize & Resist Manipulation
themes.why: Covert influence appears in relationships, workplaces, politics, and media — detection is universal self-defense.

themes.label: Actionable Frameworks
themes.why: Multi-step models civilians can apply without elite training or classified context.

themes.label: Universal Warning Signals
themes.why: High-signal warnings about patterns that destroy autonomy, trust, and judgment at scale.

S.label: Tier S — Essential
S.description: Every civilian benefits — manipulation defense, identity, media literacy, core warnings.

cards.title: Feed Sorted by Arousal
cards.nhiVerdict: Foundational mental model for media & information literacy. Changes what you notice before you react.
cards.contentPreview: Your feed isn't ordered by truth, relevance, or interest. It is sorted by emotional arousal. Algorithms test millions of variations and retain only what keeps you engaged.

cards.contentPreview: The FATE model reveals four genetically inherited triggers that drive human behavior and compliance based on ancestral survival needs.

personalDigest.title: AI NHI — Personal Digest Methodology
personalDigest.principle: 645 cards is a reference library, not a reading list. NHI routes each person through concern-based filtering → a 12–25 card personal digest → optional learning paths → full corpus on demand.

layers.name: Concern routing
layers.description: Individual selects what matters now — feeds, relationships, work, identity, civic literacy.

layers.name: Personal digest
layers.description: NHI merges concern-ranked cards with Tier S baseline — typically 12–25 cards, not 645.

layers.name: Learning paths
layers.description: Curated 5–20 minute sequences with ordered steps and one-line rationale per card.

layers.name: Full corpus
layers.description: Search, filter, compact list — entire library when you need depth or research.

methodologyDistill.lead: Smarter corpus access: relevance first, volume never.
rules: Never default to 645 — default to personalized digest
Tier S baseline always included — immunity warnings are universal
Learning paths are ordered sequences, not tag soup
Full corpus remains one click away for researchers
antiPatterns: Flat grid of 645 cards as the primary experience
Video-first navigation — cards are the knowledge
One-size Tier list without individual concern routing
Hiding 525+ cards behind arbitrary display caps

onboarding.title: NHI Concern Onboarding
onboarding.description: Six questions route you to the right concerns — not all 645 cards.

questions.question: How much do social media or news feeds shape your mood, outrage, or beliefs?

options.label: Sometimes — I catch it after the fact

questions.question: Are you navigating manipulation, gaslighting, or trust erosion in close relationships?

options.label: Watchful — want to recognize early signs

questions.question: Do authority, status, or persuasion dynamics at work affect your decisions or stress?

options.label: High — boss, team, or politics dominate

questions.question: Do groups, beliefs, or social pressure threaten your sense of who you are?
When stressed, do shame, fear, or insults make you comply when you otherwise wouldn't?
How much do propaganda, division, or manufactured consensus appear in your information diet?

options.label: Constant — hard to trust any narrative

concerns.label: Social media & news feeds
concerns.description: Algorithms, outrage loops, manufactured consensus, identity curation online.

concerns.label: Relationships & trust
concerns.description: Covert manipulation, gaslighting patterns, boundaries, and emotional exploitation.

concerns.label: Work & leadership dynamics
concerns.description: Authority, status games, persuasion in professional settings, power leaks.

followUp.question: What workplace dynamic concerns you most?

concerns.label: Identity & autonomy
concerns.description: Who you think you are — cult pressure, conformity, self-authorship under attack.

followUp.question: Where is identity pressure coming from?

concerns.label: Emotional triggers & stress
concerns.description: Shame, fear, trauma responses — what makes you compliant when pressured.

followUp.question: Which emotional lever hits you hardest?

concerns.label: Civic & information environment
concerns.description: Psyops, propaganda, division strategies, narrative warfare at population scale.

followUp.question: What civic information threat worries you?

concerns.label: General defensive literacy
concerns.description: Balanced entry — highest universal-value cards across all themes.

followUp.question: Are you new to influence defense and want a balanced starting point?
